title: Cherry Bakewell traybake
subtitle: Shortcrust base with almond frangipane and glacé cherries
excerpt: A traybake of thin shortcrust pastry, a layer of cherry jam, almond frangipane, and halved glacé cherries. Cut into squares. The frangipane sets firm and slightly crusty on top.
sourceNotes: The Bakewell tart is documented as a Derbyshire speciality, named after the town of Bakewell. The traybake format (a rectangular tin rather than a round tart tin) is a practical domestic adaptation. The glacé cherry is a twentieth-century addition to the traditional almond and jam combination.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze cut squares in a sealed box. Thaw at room temperature.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps for 4 days in a tin. The pastry stays crisp for 2 days.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven, middle shelf — the pastry base is blind-baked first at 190°C fan before adding the filling

glossaryTerms.definition: An almond cream filling made from ground almonds, butter, sugar, and eggs. When baked, it sets into a dense, moist layer with a slightly crusty surface. Distinct from marzipan (raw, not baked) and almond sponge (contains more flour). It is the filling in Bakewell tart, pithivier, and galette des rois.

content.text: This traybake has three layers: a thin shortcrust base, a layer of cherry jam, and an almond
filling. The pastry is blind-baked first to keep the base from going soggy. The frangipane bakes into a firm, slightly crusty layer that cuts cleanly.

Rub the cold {{unsalted-butter}} (for the pastry) into the {{plain-flour}}, {{icing-sugar}}, and {{fine-sea-salt}} until the mixture resembles coarse sand. Add the egg yolk and 1–2 tablespoons of cold water; mix until the dough just comes together. Flatten into a disc, wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Heat the oven to 190°C fan. Roll the pastry to fit the 20×30 cm tin, press into the base and sides, and trim the excess. Line with baking paper and fill with {{baking-beans}}. Bake for 12 minutes, then remove the beans and paper and bake for a further 5 minutes until the base is dry and just beginning to colour. Reduce the oven to 170°C fan.

Spread the {{cherry-jam}} over the warm pastry base. Beat the {{unsalted-butter}} (for the filling) and {{caster-sugar}} until pale. Beat in the {{free-range-eggs}} and {{almond-extract}}. Fold in the {{ground-almonds}}. Spread over the jam in an even layer. Press the halved {{glace-cherries}} into the surface.
Bake at 170°C fan for 30–35 minutes until the frangipane is set, golden, and a skewer inserted in the centre comes out clean. Cool completely in the tin before cutting.
